channel.title: Ming Li's Group
channel.description: Recent content on Ming Li's Group

item.title: About
item.description: About Me I received B.S. degree from Anhui University in 2009. Then, I studied the statistical physics as applied to complex networks and complex systems at University of Science and Technology of China, and received Ph.D. degree in 2014. From 2014 to 2016, I was a Post-doctoral Fellower in the Department of Modern Physics at University of Science and Technology of China. In the first half of 2016, I worked at Department of Applied Physics, The Hong Kong Polytechnic University as a Post-doctoral Fellower.

item.title: Contact
item.description: If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me. Affilication Department of Physics, Hefei University of Technology, Hefei, Anhui 230009, P. R. China Email Email: lim@hfut.edu.cn Social Google Scholar ResearchGate

item.title: Members
item.description: PI Prof. Ming Li Graduate Students Name Join Date Email Honors & Awards Qi Wang 2025.9 Xuewei Zhao 2023.9 2025 National Scholarship 2026 Outstanding Graduate (Provincial & University Level) Undergraduate Students Name Join Date Email Honor Guanyao Chen 2024.9 Yuanzhe Jiang 2024.9 Alumni Name Remarks Time in Group Degree Awarded Post-Graduation Destination Chenglei Chen Undergraduate student 2024.9-2025.12 – Another group Liwenying Yang 2024 National Scholarship 2022.9-2025.6 Master of Physics New Oriental Zhenyu Yang jointly with Dr.

item.title: News
item.description: News By employing the Conformal Loop Ensemble (CLE), we solve the iterative bicolored percolation model proposed in our prior work [Phys.Rev.Res.] for both the O(n) loop and the Fuzzy Potts models. For details, refer to Physical Review E. 2026-03-14 Our new paper on the Backbone three-point correlation function in the two-dimensional Potts model has been selected as Editors’ Suggestion in Physical Review E. 2025-11-21 We have found that the paths of depth-first search exhibits nontrivial fractal scaling across statistical physics models and all studied dimensions.

item.title: Publications
item.description: Since 2011, I have published 59 papers in collaboration with others in peer-reviewed journals. These publications include: Physics Reports (1), Physical Review Letters (1), National Science Review (1), Physical Review Research (3), Physical Review E (8), New Journal of Physics (2), Journal of Statistical Mechanics (4), Chaos (1), Chaos, Solitons & Fractals (3), Europhysics Letters (3), Physics Letters A (1), PLoS ONE (1), Scientific Reports (3), Physica A (8), Chinese Physics Letters (1), Chinese Physics B (5), Advances in Complex Systems (1), International Journal of Modern Physics C (2), Computer Networks (1), IEEE Transactions (1), conference proceedings (4), and journals in Chinese (4).

item.title: Research
item.description: Statistical Physics and Complex Systems My research lies at the intersection of statistical physics and complex systems, where I investigate the emergent behavior of interacting systems through the lens of phase transitions and critical phenomena. By leveraging analytical and computational tools, I aim to uncover universal principles governing the collective dynamics of systems ranging from simple lattice models to intricate real-world networks. Phase Transitions and Critical Phenomena Phase transitions, where a system undergoes a qualitative change in its macroscopic properties, are a central theme in statistical physics.

item.title: Teaching
item.description: 2026春季(Spring term 2026) 本科生课程(for undergraduate students): 热力学与统计物理 (Thermodynamics and Statistical Physics) 2025秋季(Autumn term 2025) 本科生课程(for undergraduate students): 新生研讨课 (Freshman Seminar) 一次报告(Contribute a lecture) 研究生课程(for graduate students): 学科前沿专题 (Lectures on Frontiers of the Discipline) 一次报告(Contribute a lecture) 2025春季(Spring term 2025) 本科生课程(for undergraduate students): 热力学与统计物理 (Thermodynamics and Statistical Physics) 2024秋季(Autumn term 2024) 本科生课程(for undergraduate students): 新生研讨课 (Freshman Seminar) 一次报告(Contribute a lecture) 研究生课程(for graduate students): 学科前沿专题 (Lectures on Frontiers of the Discipline) 一次报告(Contribute a lecture)

item.title: 中文
item.description: 基本情况 李明，硕士/博士生导师，研究员/黄山学者特聘教授，合肥工业大学物理学院物理系。 本研究组主要开展统计物理与复杂系统方向的理论与数值模拟研究，关注多体系统中从微观相互作用到宏观集体行为的涌现过程。当前研究兴趣包括临界现象的动态与几何分析方法、高维系统中的相变问题，以及复杂网络的结构与动力学特性。主要研究成果请见Publications或者谷歌学术。 招生情况 如果你对统计物理、计算物理或编程模拟感兴趣，并具备一定基础，或希望在科研过程中逐步提升数理分析与计算能力，欢迎加入我们，一起探索有趣的科学问题。欢迎通过邮件联系交流。lim@hfut.edu.cn。
